description: Bid 2C over 1NT as Garbage Stayman — a weak-hand escape with both majors, including the Crawling Stayman continuation when diamonds are short
purpose: Use Stayman as an escape mechanism with weak hands containing both majors, passing a safe response or rebidding 2H over a 2D denial when diamonds are short

authority.label: Wikipedia (Stayman convention) — Garbage Stayman and crawling Stayman variants

teaching.tradeoff: Garbage Stayman sacrifices the standard HCP requirement for Stayman to let weak both-major hands escape 1NT.
teaching.principle: With a weak hand and both majors, use Stayman to escape 1NT; pass a safe response, or over a 2D denial bid 2H when diamonds are too short to pass.

commonMistakes.text: Do not use Garbage Stayman without both majors.
commonMistakes.reason: The ask only works when every likely major reply is a safe resting place.

commonMistakes.text: Do not pass opener's 2D denial with short diamonds.
commonMistakes.reason: When diamonds are short, Crawling Stayman uses 2H to ask opener to choose a major instead.

commonMistakes.text: Do not treat Garbage Stayman as constructive.
commonMistakes.reason: The weak-hand escape and the value-asking version solve different problems.

explanationEntries.displayText: Eligible for Stayman
Stayman is the preferred convention

clauses.rationale: Garbage Stayman is for weak hands only
must be safe if opener bids 2♥
must be safe if opener bids 2♠
short clubs ensures 4+ diamonds as escape after 2♦ denial

teachingLabel.summary: Escape 1NT with a weak hand (0–7 HCP) holding both majors and short clubs — plan to pass any response

clauses.rationale: use Stayman to find the 4-4 fit

clauses.rationale: Crawling Stayman is a weak-hand rescue sequence
Opener may pass 2H as the chosen major
Opener may correct to 2S as the chosen major
Responder cannot safely pass opener's 2D denial

teachingLabel.name: Crawling Stayman 2♥
teachingLabel.summary: After opener's 2♦ denial, bid 2♥ with a weak 4-4 majors hand and short diamonds so opener can pass hearts or correct to spades

clauses.rationale: Garbage Stayman: pass any response with a weak hand
Garbage Stayman asks only with short clubs
Passing 2D needs a diamond length to make the contract playable

teachingLabel.name: Pass (Garbage Stayman)
teachingLabel.summary: Pass opener's 2♦ denial — with a weak hand and 4+ diamonds (from short clubs shape), this is a safe partscore

summaryCard.trigger: Partner opens one notrump and responder is escaping
summaryCard.partnership: Requires an explicit partnership agreement that Stayman can be non-forcing here; responder uses the two-club ask as a weak escape, with Crawling Stayman available after a diamond denial.

whenToUse.gloss: Use it only with a weak hand.
Use it when both majors can absorb any forced partscore.
Use it when short clubs make a diamond escape safe.
After a diamond denial, rebid two hearts as Crawling Stayman when diamonds are short.

workedAuctions.label: Heart reply becomes a safe signoff

calls.rationale: Opener shows a balanced notrump opening.
Responder uses Garbage Stayman with a weak hand, both majors, and short clubs.
Opener shows hearts, giving responder the expected escape hatch.
Responder passes because the whole point was to escape one notrump into a playable major partscore.

workedAuctions.label: Spade reply still lands in safety

calls.rationale: Opener starts with the balanced notrump opening.
Responder asks with the weak escape hand, planning to pass any answer.
Opener denies hearts first and then shows the four-card spade suit.
Responder signs off immediately because the spade fit is already located.

workedAuctions.label: Diamond denial supplies the fallback partscore

calls.rationale: Opener begins with the notrump opening.
Responder uses the artificial ask as an escape route, not as a value ask.
Opener denies a four-card major.
Short clubs imply enough diamonds to tolerate the denial as the final spot.

workedAuctions.label: Crawling Stayman avoids a short-diamond trap

calls.rationale: Opener begins with the notrump opening.
Responder starts the weak escape with both majors.
Opener denies a four-card major.
With short diamonds, responder crawls to hearts and asks opener to pass hearts or correct to spades.
Opener chooses spades at the cheapest level when that is the better major.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: both-four-card majors with only two diamonds gets trapped in a thin diamond fit

calls.rationale: Opener starts with the balanced notrump opening.
Responder misuses Garbage Stayman with a 4-4-2-3 hand that lacks the short-club, long-diamond escape pattern.
Opener denies a four-card major, which is the danger case.
Passing strands the partnership in a 4-2 diamond fit, exactly the failure mode Garbage Stayman is supposed to avoid.

items.ourAction: Garbage Stayman is often off while rescue methods take over.
items.note: Confirm whether the partnership keeps systems on before using the artificial ask.

items.ourAction: The escape may survive through a cue bid if the partnership keeps systems on.
items.note: Without that agreement, compete naturally and keep the weak hand simple.

items.ourAction: The garbage route is off and responder competes naturally.
items.note: Once the room for the ask disappears, there is no artificial escape left to show.
There is no major escape to look for.
A single major does not justify the ask.
With five-four majors, show the five-card major immediately; Garbage Stayman is for the weak both-majors escape, not the transfer hand.
Flat shape offers no trump gain.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Standard Stayman promises invitational-plus values; Garbage Stayman escapes a weak hand with both four-card majors.
Smolen starts from the same ask but continues with game-forcing five-four majors.
Transfers handle weak hands with a real five-card major, including the five-four major patterns that should not start with Garbage Stayman.

definitions.description: Eligible for Stayman (4+ card major AND 8+ HCP)
Stayman preferred (eligible AND either no 5-card major OR 5-4 in both majors)
Posterior probability that N/S have an 8+ card major fit
Posterior probability that opener has balanced shape
Posterior probability that opener has a second 4-card major
